limitation_status: Shape-parity scaffold, not IPC paper parity. The rows cover only scenes DART can run today with the mass-spring/FEM barrier solver; the paper-scale scenes (e.g. Fig. 22, 688K nodes) and the head-to-head IPC Table-1 CPU comparison against the published reference numbers remain blocked on the M4 upstream asset pipeline (no upstream meshes are vendored). This packet proves the Fig. 23 statistics SHAPE (per-scene solver/scaling and contact axes) is emitted and machine-checkable, not that any figure is reproduced.
invariant_semantics: Each row's 'invariant' is a human-readable description of scene intent. The machine-checked gates are: metric_fields (present, finite, non-negative), positive_fields (a subset of metric_fields required strictly > 0), and zero_fields (extra counters required present, finite, and exactly 0). Cross-solver equilibrium parity (that direct/CG/matrix-free settle to the SAME configuration) is proven by the C++ regressions in test_deformable_body, not by this packet.

families.solver-scaling: Contact-free FEM scenes exercising the projected-Newton per-step cost axis (Newton iterations/step, CG iterations/residual, assembled sparse-Hessian footprint) and per-step wall time.
families.contact-statistics: Self-contact barrier scenes exercising the IPC Fig. 23 contact axis (converged and peak active-contact counts, minimum active-contact distance).

rows.invariant: Neo-Hookean FEM cantilever on the sparse-direct projected-Newton solver reports a positive per-step Newton-iteration count and a positive assembled sparse-Hessian footprint.
Fixed-corotational FEM cantilever (exact analytic FCR Hessian) on the sparse-direct path reports a positive per-step Newton-iteration count and a positive assembled sparse-Hessian footprint.

The FEM cantilever on the incomplete-Cholesky CG path reports positive per-step CG iterations and a non-negative residual estimate while still assembling the sparse Hessian for the preconditioner (positive footprint).

The matrix-free CG path applies local Hessian blocks with block-Jacobi preconditioning; it reports positive per-step CG and matrix-free solve counts and, by design, assembles no sparse Hessian (enforced: zero hessian_nonzeros and hessian_storage_bytes).
Solid N^3 FEM cube (wide Hessian bandwidth) on the sparse-direct path reports a positive assembled Hessian footprint that grows with the 3D fill-in; the direct-vs-iterative crossover fixture.
The chunky 3D cube on the incomplete-Cholesky CG path reports positive per-step CG iterations and a non-negative residual estimate; its per-step cost stays near O(nnz) as the mesh chunks up.

A surface folding onto itself keeps a strictly positive minimum active-contact distance (enforced > 0, the IPC intersection-free statistic) with positive converged and peak active-contact counts inside the barrier activation band.
